mnemonics: P.A.R.T.Y. - Processes, Availability, Replication, Timing, Yet another consensus (for core distributed systems concepts evaluated continuously)
keyPoints: Continuous evaluation focuses on ongoing assessment of system health and performance metrics.
Key metrics include latency, throughput, fault tolerance, and consistency guarantees.
The goal is to proactively identify and mitigate issues before they cause significant system failure.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on end-to-end latency and ignoring partial failures or degraded performance in specific system partitions.
Assuming a synchronous system model and not accounting for network delays and partitions in evaluation.
Evaluating availability only at the client-facing layer and ignoring the health of internal, dependent services.
